ideas.title: Google Search Just Became an AI Assistant
ideas.about: Google I/O pushed AI Search/AI Mode into the center of the conversation. Braydon should explain what this means in plain English: people are not just searching links anymore, they are asking Google to reason, compare, plan, and do the first pass of work.
ideas.evidence: Google News surfaced Google's own I/O 2026 roundup, "Welcome to the agentic Gemini era," on May 19. The Verge also published a same-day "13 biggest announcements" roundup. Marcin Teodoru posted a fresh Google I/O clip titled "@Google I/O Exclusive AI Search is LIVE" on May 19 with the spoken hook: "as promised I got the exclusive Robbie Stein VP of product at Google Search... breaking news update of everything that just got launched today at Google I/O." It is early at 2.4K views, so use it as current news context, not as proven hook performance.
ideas.onScreenTitle: Google Search is not just search anymore
ideas.spokenHook: If you run a business, this Google update matters because your customers may stop clicking through ten blue links.
ideas.why: It turns a giant announcement into a practical owner-level question: how will people find answers, vendors, and local businesses now? Frank can understand that in 5 seconds.

ideas.title: Gemini Video Is the New Demo Hook, Not Just a Toy
ideas.about: Use Google Gemini/Omni video news as a simple business demo: take one boring business idea and show how AI turns it into a short visual concept, ad, explainer, or product scene.
ideas.evidence: The Google I/O news wave includes Gemini and video announcements. Marcin Teodoru posted a May 20 Gemini clip titled "@googlegemini Omni Video is here..." with the spoken hook: "Looking at you." It is too early and low at 670 views, but it confirms creators are already trying to package the Google video update. Braydon's own visual-output posts are the stronger proof: Claude cartoon videos at 236.7K and Claude plus Higgsfield at 204.8K.
ideas.onScreenTitle: I turned one boring business idea into a video
ideas.spokenHook: Google just made AI video way more useful, so I tested it on the least exciting business example I could think of.
ideas.why: Braydon's audience already rewards visible AI output. This is a Google news version of the exact pattern that is winning on his account.

ideas.title: Claude's Small-Business Agent, But Give It a Real Job
ideas.about: Do a follow-up to the small-business-agent post, but make it less announcement and more "watch me delegate one boring task." Examples: turn messy notes into a client follow-up, build a simple invoice checklist, compare three vendor options, or draft a weekly owner dashboard.
ideas.evidence: Braydon's Claude small-business-agent post is now at **42.9K views**, 540 shares, and 1,757 saves. Nocode Joshua also posted a related Claude small-business/plugin clip on May 19 titled "Claude drops new AI Free plugin for $0.00" with 3.9K views and a verified spoken hook: "Claude just dropped the craziest new plugin and it's completely free.
ideas.onScreenTitle: I gave Claude one boring business task
ideas.spokenHook: This is what Claude's small-business agent actually does when you give it a real job, not a hype prompt.
ideas.why: Braydon already has proof that this topic is climbing. The V2 angle answers the comment-level question people always have after a tool announcement: "okay, but what do I do with it?

ideas.title: Stop Asking AI to Run Your Money
ideas.about: Reframe the finance AI update around safe judgment: use AI to organize questions, spot confusing categories, and prep for an accountant, not to make final financial decisions.
ideas.evidence: Braydon's newest Claude finance plugin post is early at 1,965 views. Recent high-signal AI conversation also includes ChatGPT's finance experience from the May 19 scan history. The safer, mentor-style angle is stronger than a raw tool demo.
ideas.onScreenTitle: Do not let AI run your money
ideas.spokenHook: Please do not let AI make your money decisions. But it can help you ask way better questions before you call your accountant.
ideas.why: It is clear, responsible, and booker-friendly. It gives a practical use case without sounding like financial advice.

competitors.creator: sabrina_ramonov
competitors.text: Follow and comment "MUFFIN" and I'll send you all my free AI education 👇 If you're sitting on the sidelines of this AI gold rush, you're making these 5 mistakes. There is massive opportunity but people overcomplicate it. 1. Stop obsessing over AI agents and automations on day 1. Those come last. 2. You don't have to start an AI business. Take a proven model you already understand and apply AI to the tedious parts. 3. Focus where demand is massive. Claude grew 80x last quarter. Copilot has millions of paid users with no idea how to use it. 4. Pick ONE thing, not 5. You won't do 5 things well. 5. Stop paying for courses. Stop watching my videos. Build, ship, reach out, put yourself out there. Action beats consumption. How do you make $100k with AI in 2026? What is the biggest AI opportunity right now? Why do most people fail to monetize AI? #ai #makemoneyonline #aitools #entrepreneur #startup

competitors.creator: sabrina_ramonov
competitors.text: Follow and comment "HOPE" and I'll send you my free AI courses 👇 The people getting rich from AI right now aren't the smartest. They share 9 habits and most people fail at habit 1. I sold my AI company for millions at 30. Now I teach AI for free. Here's what matters: 1. Ask AI before Google, before a friend, before paying a guru. Every time. 2. Verify everything. A lawyer cited 3 fake cases from ChatGPT. 3. Context is the skill. "Write me a plan" gets garbage. Add your goal, audience, constraints. 4. Use AI to prep before meetings with experts, accountants, lawyers. 5. Train AI like a new hire. Give feedback every day. By month 3 it's dialed in. 6. End every session asking AI what you should have prompted better. 7. SOPs for everything you repeat. 4 hours turns into 30 minutes. 8. Stop yapping. Connect AI to Gmail, Canva, Drive, Blotato. Let it do the work. 9. Stop trying to build an AI company. Apply AI to a proven model instead. Which 9 AI skills will make you rich in 2026? How do top earners use AI daily? What AI habits matter most this year? #ai #aitools #productivity #makemoneyonline #entrepreneur

competitors.creator: digitalsamaritan
competitors.text: 3 months ago, I refused to make plans for the May long weekend and even declined a brand event for May 19th because Google announced I/O 2026 in Mountain View, California. At the time, I had no idea if I would ever get invited or if Google even invited creators. But when the @googlegemini team started commenting on some of my videos on Instagram and TikTok, I took it as a sign to shoot my shot. I emailed every contact I had, asking if they had any creator programs and pitching myself for an invite to this event. Long story short, guess who got an official invite to go to Google I/O in California next week? I grew up watching big tech YouTubers going to these events and never in a million years imagined that one day I would be one of them. Feel surreal!

Here's the fastest fix so in thropic the creators of Claude advise that after 20 or 30 messages with Claude you start a brand new conversation but the problem is when you transfer to a new conversation sometimes Claude leaves out important details and so I I created a new skill called the handoff skill that makes sure that Claude captures all the important information and seamlessly transfers it to any new conversation and this handoff skill generates the summary using the exact format below and it's able to capture the core contexts key decisions what's pending and the tone and style of that conversation and then what it does is that it creates this beautiful hand off summary that it can feed directly inside of the next Claude conversation so now whenever you have a really long conversation inside of Claude all you need to do is type in this skill forward slash hand off and it will generate this perfect hand off summary that you can then copy and put into a brand new conversation with Claude to ensure that you start exactly where you left off without losing any context at all so if you want this exact handoff skill and a full guide on the best Claude skills and how to optimize your Claude setup just comment the word Claude and I'll go ahead and send it over to you and as always if you like this sort Of content and you want to stay ahead in the world of AI, make sure that you give me a follow.

competitors.creator: taki.gpt
competitors.text: Comment “CLAUDE” to get the best Claude optimization and setup guide on the internet. Boris Cherny created Claude Code and he just shared 10 tips on how he uses it. Here are the key takeaways. First, always start with Plan Mode. Do this before any code. Just press Shift+Tab to turn it on. Iterate on your plan. Make it perfect. This stops many errors later. Think of it like drawing a map first. Second, run multiple Claude Code sessions. Use each for a different project part. Then merge them at the end. Let AI do the merge. You finish projects three to five times faster. It’s like having extra hands working at once. Third, the agent makes a mistake? Tell it to update its own claude.md file. Do this every time. It learns. The same mistake won’t happen again. Simple as fixing a recipe note. Fourth, build sub-agents. Use them to automate your work. One agent writes code. Another reviews it. A third deploys it. They all run on their own. Picture a team passing a baton smoothly. The good news? These tips fit any workflow. Because anytime Claude helps with one part, it speeds up the rest: #borischerny #claudeai #claudecode #claudetips #aitipsandtricks
competitors.topic: 10 Pro Tips for Claude Code / From the creator of Claude Code!
competitors.onScreenTitle: 10 Pro Tips for Claude Code / From the creator of Claude Code!
competitors.spokenHook: The creator of Claude Code Boris Cherny just dropped 10 tips on how he uses Claude Code and here are the key takeaways I've personally tried all of them and I'll show you the top five of his tips if you want to get the most out of Claude and stay till the end because the last one is definitely the most impactful and if you like this sort of content and you want to get the most out of your a I experience make sure that you give me a follow tip number one is that he runs multiple Claude agents in parallel inside of his terminal and inside of the Claude desktop app it self and running multiple agents is useful because you can have each agent take care of specific responsibilities like one for coding one for testing one for design one for iteration and one for review tip No. 2 is he uses a shared Claude MD file which is basically a context file that teaches Claude everything about who he is, how he gets work done, his business etc and when you share that with your entire team everybody then has access to that same context so the results that they're gonna create are gonna be similar tip No.

briefMarkdown:
*Generated: May 20, 2026 at 6:39 AM MT*

# TikTok Ideas, May 20, 2026

**Strategist read.** Post from Google today, but do not make it a conference recap. The slot Braydon can own is: "Google just changed search, video, and AI agents. Here is what a normal business owner should actually do with that." The best evidence is a live Google I/O news wave, Marcin Teodoru already posting fresh Google I/O clips, and Braydon's own account showing that plainspoken Claude demos with visible outputs are still the strongest format. Lead with one Google Search or Gemini video idea, then use Braydon's strong Claude small-business-agent post as the fallback if Google feels too noisy.

## Performance Check-In

- **Top current Braydon post:** "Claude now makes cartoon videos" is at **236.7K views**, 10.1K likes, 2,292 shares, and 12,987 saves. Visual AI demos are still the winning lane.
- **Second monster visual proof:** "Claude can now make amazing images by connecting it to Higgsfield" is at **204.8K views**, 1,959 shares, and 10,325 saves.
- **Strong fresh climb:** "Claude just released a small business agent" is at **42.9K views**, 540 shares, and 1,757 saves. That is the best current V2 signal.
- **Healthy middle signal:** "Claude can now connect to Canva" is at **15.6K views** and 444 saves.
- **Newest posts:** Claude finance plugin is at 1,965 views and Claude memory trick is at 1,340 views. They are early, but not yet showing the same lift as visible-output demos.

## Idea 1: Google Search Just Became an AI Assistant

`📰 news` `🌊 wave-ride`

- **What it's about:** Google I/O pushed AI Search/AI Mode into the center of the conversation. Braydon should explain what this means in plain English: people are not just searching links anymore, they are asking Google to reason, compare, plan, and do the first pass of work.
- **Evidence:** Google News surfaced Google's own I/O 2026 roundup, "Welcome to the agentic Gemini era," on May 19. The Verge also published a same-day "13 biggest announcements" roundup. Marcin Teodoru posted a fresh Google I/O clip titled "@Google I/O Exclusive AI Search is LIVE" on May 19 with the spoken hook: "as promised I got the exclusive Robbie Stein VP of product at Google Search... breaking news update of everything that just got launched today at Google I/O." It is early at 2.4K views, so use it as current news context, not as proven hook performance.
- **On-screen title:** "Google Search is not just search anymore"
- **Spoken hook:** "If you run a business, this Google update matters because your customers may stop clicking through ten blue links."
- **Content type:** news | myth-bust
- **Why this should work:** It turns a giant announcement into a practical owner-level question: how will people find answers, vendors, and local businesses now? Frank can understand that in 5 seconds.

## Idea 2: Gemini Video Is the New Demo Hook, Not Just a Toy

`📰 news` `🌊 wave-ride` `🔁 V2/reframe`

- **What it's about:** Use Google Gemini/Omni video news as a simple business demo: take one boring business idea and show how AI turns it into a short visual concept, ad, explainer, or product scene.
- **Evidence:** The Google I/O news wave includes Gemini and video announcements. Marcin Teodoru posted a May 20 Gemini clip titled "@googlegemini Omni Video is here..." with the spoken hook: "Looking at you." It is too early and low at 670 views, but it confirms creators are already trying to package the Google video update. Braydon's own visual-output posts are the stronger proof: Claude cartoon videos at 236.7K and Claude plus Higgsfield at 204.8K.
- **On-screen title:** "I turned one boring business idea into a video"
- **Spoken hook:** "Google just made AI video way more useful, so I tested it on the least exciting business example I could think of."
- **Content type:** tutorial | tool breakdown
- **Why this should work:** Braydon's audience already rewards visible AI output. This is a Google news version of the exact pattern that is winning on his account.

## Idea 3: Claude's Small-Business Agent, But Give It a Real Job

`🔁 V2/reframe` `🌊 wave-ride`

- **What it's about:** Do a follow-up to the small-business-agent post, but make it less announcement and more "watch me delegate one boring task." Examples: turn messy notes into a client follow-up, build a simple invoice checklist, compare three vendor options, or draft a weekly owner dashboard.
- **Evidence:** Braydon's Claude small-business-agent post is now at **42.9K views**, 540 shares, and 1,757 saves. Nocode Joshua also posted a related Claude small-business/plugin clip on May 19 titled "Claude drops new AI Free plugin for $0.00" with 3.9K views and a verified spoken hook: "Claude just dropped the craziest new plugin and it's completely free."
- **On-screen title:** "I gave Claude one boring business task"
- **Spoken hook:** "This is what Claude's small-business agent actually does when you give it a real job, not a hype prompt."
- **Content type:** tutorial
- **Why this should work:** Braydon already has proof that this topic is climbing. The V2 angle answers the comment-level question people always have after a tool announcement: "okay, but what do I do with it?"

## Idea 4: Stop Asking AI to Run Your Money

`🔁 V2/reframe` `📰 news`

- **What it's about:** Reframe the finance AI update around safe judgment: use AI to organize questions, spot confusing categories, and prep for an accountant, not to make final financial decisions.
- **Evidence:** Braydon's newest Claude finance plugin post is early at 1,965 views. Recent high-signal AI conversation also includes ChatGPT's finance experience from the May 19 scan history. The safer, mentor-style angle is stronger than a raw tool demo.
- **On-screen title:** "Do not let AI run your money"
- **Spoken hook:** "Please do not let AI make your money decisions. But it can help you ask way better questions before you call your accountant."
- **Content type:** myth-bust | quick tip
- **Why this should work:** It is clear, responsible, and booker-friendly. It gives a practical use case without sounding like financial advice.

## Today's Posting Order

1. **Post Idea 1 first if you can shoot a clean, timely Google Search take.** This is the freshest May 20 news slot.
2. **Post Idea 2 if you can show a visual before/after.** A visible Google video demo is more likely to match Braydon's 200K-view visual-output pattern.
3. **Post Idea 3 as the safest performance-backed fallback.** The Claude small-business-agent post is already proving demand.
4. **Keep Idea 4 as a later quick tip.** It is responsible and useful, but current scrape data says visual demos are outperforming finance/memory tips.

## Why these ideas and not something else

- I did not recommend a generic "13 Google I/O announcements" recap because Braydon wins when he translates one change into one obvious business action.
- I did not treat Marcin's newest Google clips as proven viral evidence yet. They are current news signals with extracted titles and hooks, but views are still early.
- I did not recycle yesterday's Claude Canva idea as a top slot because Braydon already has a stronger current V2 signal in the Claude small-business-agent post.
- I kept the finance angle cautious because Braydon's brand is mentor, not hype guru, and financial advice needs plain boundaries.

## Research Notes

### Competitor signals

- **New competitor posts found:** 5 newly seen recent posts across Sabrina Ramonov, Marcin Teodoru, and Nocode Joshua.
- **Strongest new competitor performance:** Sabrina's "If you're not making $100,000+ in AI gold rush... stop making these mistakes" has 11.7K views, 552 saves, and a verified spoken hook about people overcomplicating the AI gold rush. It is a strong pattern, but the money/gold-rush framing is not Braydon's cleanest brand fit.
- **Google-specific competitor signal:** Marcin posted two Google I/O clips. Titles extracted: "@googlegemini Omni Video is here..." and "@Google I/O Exclusive AI Search is LIVE." These are news-context signals, not 10K+ performance proof yet.
- **Claude small-business signal:** Nocode Joshua's Claude free plugin clip is at 3.9K views with title and spoken hook extracted. Braydon's own 42.9K post is much stronger proof for the same topic lane.

### Google/news opportunities

- Google I/O is the one-time priority that earned a slot today. Google News RSS surfaced Google's own May 19 I/O 2026 article, "Welcome to the agentic Gemini era," and The Verge's same-day roundup.
- Best Braydon-friendly framing: "what changed for normal business owners," not "here are all the announcements."
- The clearest angles are AI Search changing discovery, and Gemini video/Flow turning business ideas into visual assets.

### Own-performance context

- Braydon's account is still rewarding visible AI outputs far more than abstract AI advice.
- Claude small-business agent is the best fresh non-visual proof at 42.9K views with high shares/saves.
- Newest finance and memory posts are not dead, but they are not the lead today unless they start climbing later.

## Bullet-Point Scripts

### Idea 1: Google Search Just Became an AI Assistant

- Open with: "Google Search is not just search anymore."
- Show a simple example: instead of searching five links, ask for a comparison, a plan, or a shortlist.
- Explain the business implication: customers may get their first answer before they ever click your website.
- Give the owner action: make your website, reviews, FAQs, and examples easier for AI to understand.
- Close with: "You do not need to panic. You need to make your business easier to explain."

### Idea 2: Gemini Video Is the New Demo Hook, Not Just a Toy

- Open with: "I tested Google's new AI video update on a boring business example."
- Pick one concrete business: tree farm, lawyer, real estate agent, accountant, or local service company.
- Show the prompt and the generated visual concept.
- Explain the use case: ad idea, explainer, customer education, or first draft for a real video.
- Close with: "The win is not replacing your creative team. It is getting from blank page to first draft faster."

### Idea 3: Claude's Small-Business Agent, But Give It a Real Job

- Open with: "This is what Claude's small-business agent does with an actual business task."
- Give it messy input: notes, a client request, vendor options, or an owner checklist.
- Show the output: organized next steps, email draft, dashboard, or simple SOP.
- Say where a human still checks it.
- Close with: "That is the point. AI does the first pass, you bring the judgment."

## Sources

- Google News RSS result: "I/O 2026: Welcome to the agentic Gemini era" from blog.google, published May 19, 2026.
- Google News RSS result: "The 13 biggest announcements at Google I/O 2026" from The Verge, published May 19, 2026.
- Competitor artifact: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/tmp/2026-05-20-0616-cron-run/competitors_compact.json`
- Braydon artifact: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/tmp/2026-05-20-0616-cron-run/braydon_compact.json`
- Run summary: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/tmp/2026-05-20-0616-cron-run/summary.json`
- Hook cache: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/data/tiktok-hooks-cache.json`
- Performance log: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/data/tiktok-performance-log.md`

## Run notes and limitations

- Competitor scrape returned 50 posts across all 10 configured handles: sabrina_ramonov, rileybrown.ai, tony.aube, the.rachelwoods, nocode.joshua, marcinteodoru, taki.gpt, digitalsamaritan, brandnat, and justyn.ai.
- New competitor posts found before cache writes: 5.
- New-post on-screen title coverage: 5/5.
- New-post spoken-hook coverage: 5/5.
- New-post complete title plus hook coverage: 5/5. This reached Braydon's 100% new-post extraction requirement.
- Recent 7 to 10 day competitor-window coverage: 34/37 on-screen titles, 32/37 spoken hooks, 32/37 complete title plus hook.
- No post-level new-post extraction failures remained after cover download, vision title extraction, and VTT spoken-hook parsing.
- Google/X research note: X scrape returned some Google-related results but the strongest fresh Google evidence for this run came from Google News RSS and current competitor Google I/O posts. The tweet scan also included older/high-signal AI posts, so it was used as secondary context only.
- No `NEEDS_VISION_OCR` placeholders remain in the hook cache from this run.

briefSections.introMarkdown:
**Strategist read.** Post from Google today, but do not make it a conference recap. The slot Braydon can own is: "Google just changed search, video, and AI agents. Here is what a normal business owner should actually do with that." The best evidence is a live Google I/O news wave, Marcin Teodoru already posting fresh Google I/O clips, and Braydon's own account showing that plainspoken Claude demos with visible outputs are still the strongest format. Lead with one Google Search or Gemini video idea, then use Braydon's strong Claude small-business-agent post as the fallback if Google feels too noisy.

## Performance Check-In

- **Top current Braydon post:** "Claude now makes cartoon videos" is at **236.7K views**, 10.1K likes, 2,292 shares, and 12,987 saves. Visual AI demos are still the winning lane.
- **Second monster visual proof:** "Claude can now make amazing images by connecting it to Higgsfield" is at **204.8K views**, 1,959 shares, and 10,325 saves.
- **Strong fresh climb:** "Claude just released a small business agent" is at **42.9K views**, 540 shares, and 1,757 saves. That is the best current V2 signal.
- **Healthy middle signal:** "Claude can now connect to Canva" is at **15.6K views** and 444 saves.
- **Newest posts:** Claude finance plugin is at 1,965 views and Claude memory trick is at 1,340 views. They are early, but not yet showing the same lift as visible-output demos.

strategistSummary: Account signal: visible Claude output demos are the current winners. Lead with something people can see, not an abstract AI take.
Best finance frame: AI helps you ask better money questions before you talk to your accountant, not “AI replaces finance pros.”
Best creative frame: Claude + Canva/design workflows, because they stack with both competitor momentum and Braydon’s own visual-demo wins.
